source_jobs.title: Senior Electrical Engineer, Hardware
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BRINC:

At BRINC, we are redefining public safety with an innovative ecosystem of life-saving tools. Our journey started with the development of drones and ruggedized throw phones, designed to access unsafe areas and establish communication to de-escalate situations. Today, we've expanded into creating and deploying 911 response networks, where drones are dispatched to 911 calls to provide real-time visual data, enhancing safety and enabling de-escalation-focused responses. Our cutting-edge solutions are utilized by over 600 public safety agencies across America and the company has raised over $150M from investors, including Index Ventures, Motorola Solutions, Sam Altman, Dylan Field, Mike Volpe, Alexandr Wang and more. At BRINC, we are committed to recruiting the world's best talent to join us in our mission to support first responders in saving lives.




ABOUT THIS ROLE:

We are seeking talented and experienced Senior-Principal level Electrical Engineers that can lead the conceptualization, design, and testing of BRINC’s drone systems. In this role, you will be responsible for circuit design, power distribution, sensor integration, and PCB development, ensuring our products meet high performance and reliability standards. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate electrical systems with mechanical, software, and autonomy components, playing a key role in bringing innovative technology to market.

This role demands specialized expertise in electrical engineering, but at BRINC, our Senior Engineers are also versatile problem-solvers. You’ll take ownership of our toughest technical challenges, and thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment where adaptability is critical.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Own hardware development from start to finish - from specification to design, prototype, testing and manufacturing.

 - Work collaboratively with software, mechanical, perception, controls, and manufacturing engineers.

 - Design both flight hardware and test setups to validate designs.

 - Establish top-level design architectures and implementation methodologies for the team. Clearly communicate design decisions with internal stakeholders.

 - Perform and document design analysis and testing results; make data-driven decisions to land key product features.

 - Work closely with product partners to ensure the final product meets design intent and customer needs.

 - Provide technical guidance and mentorship to the broader electrical engineering team.




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 5+ years of industry experience in electrical engineering

 - Bachelor of Science in Electrical, Computer, or related Engineering degree

 - Experience with industry standard layout & schematic capture tools and deep knowledge of PCB design best practices

 - Experience developing PCBAs for digital embedded systems (MCU, SoC, FPGA, etc.)

 - Experience designing power regulation and delivery systems

 - Experience with standard embedded interfaces (I2C, SPI, UART, etc.) as well as high-speed camera, display, and memory interfaces (MIPI, DDR, PCIe, etc.)




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - 10+ years of experience designing electronics for Aerospace, Robotics, Automotive, Consumer Electronics, or related industries

 - Masters of Science in Electrical Engineering or related degree

 - Experience developing UAV/UAS electronic systems for commercial, industrial, or defense applications

 - Experience working with domestic and international supply chain partners

 - In-depth understanding of partnering with Mechanical and Product Design engineers to design for high shock, vibration, humidity and thermal environments

 - Experience working with FPGAs (Xilinx), including FW development

 - Experience running Signal Integrity and Power Integrity simulations and knowledge of best practices




BRINC CULTURE VALUES:

 - Try the hard stuff

 - Be innovative - Invent the future

 - Move fast

 - Listen to end-users

 - Strive for excellence

 - Don’t

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BRINC:

At BRINC, we are redefining public safety with an innovative ecosystem of life-saving tools. Our journey started with the development of drones and ruggedized throw phones, designed to access unsafe areas and establish communication to de-escalate situations. Today, we've expanded into creating and deploying 911 response networks, where drones are dispatched to 911 calls to provide real-time visual data, enhancing safety and enabling de-escalation-focused responses. Our cutting-edge solutions are utilized by over 600 public safety agencies across America and the company has raised over $150M from investors, including Index Ventures, Motorola Solutions, Sam Altman, Dylan Field, Mike Volpe, Alexandr Wang and more. At BRINC, we are committed to recruiting the world's best talent to join us in our mission to support first responders in saving lives.




ABOUT THIS ROLE:

As a Production Technician, you will play a hands-on role in bringing our products to life. With foundational skills already in place, you will perform detailed work, such as precision soldering, troubleshooting and collaborating with other teams to improve processes.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Product Assembly:
   
   - Fabricate and assemble components using schematics, diagrams and written instructions.
   
   - Read and/or create assembly work instructions, repair manuals and liaise with other departments to assemble products.
   
   - Perform micro-soldering, rework and reflow tasks.
   
   - Build prototype models based on technical guidelines.
   
   - Manage and install cables, wiring, splicing as needed.

 - Testing, Troubleshooting & Quality Assurance:
   
   - Inspect, analyze and troubleshoot systems and equipment.
   
   - Run tests and interpret results to make effective recommendations.
   
   - Diagnose and repair electronic and mechanical issues in prototypes or production models.
   
   - Determine material or replacement needs and a logical method for repair; take appropriate action to meet those needs and make repairs.
   
   - Repair, install and inspect electronics or electronic systems as directed through verbal or written instructions, diagrams, drawings or specifications.
   
   - Create or adapt parts as needed to make repairs and improve the performance of various electronics.
   
   - Ensure all builds meet established quality standards and specifications.
   
   - Perform safety checks to ensure design plans are feasible in various conditions.

 - Documentation:
   
   - Maintain accurate records of assembly processes, testing results, repairs and any modifications.
   
   - Write reports, and follow safety regulations and preventative maintenance plans.
   
   - Provide feedback on design or process improvements to enhance production efficiency.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration:
   
   - Work closely with engineers and other team members to meet production goals.
   
   - Follow and contribute to safety protocols and workplace cleanliness.
     
     


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Associate degree in avionics, electronics, or a similar field; or equivalent relevant experience.

 - Ability to use hand and power tools.

 - Ability to use diagnostic tools.

 - Ability to understand and utilize work instructions and technical manuals.

 - Strong analytical skills and ability to work independently.

 - Ability to lift up to 49lbs.

 - Ability to sit, stand or be on your feet for extended periods of time.
   
   


PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Ability to troubleshoot electrical and mechanical repairs.

 - Experience fixing and maintaining drones and knowledge of different drone systems.

 - Basic knowledge of drone operation and maintenance.

 - Understanding of quality control processes and documentation.





BRINC CULTURE VALUES:

 - Try the hard stuff

 - Be innovative - Invent the future

 - Move fast

 - Listen to end-users

 - Strive for excellence

 - Don’t build a dystopia

 - Be frugal

 - Save lives through technology

source_jobs.description:

At BRINC, we are redefining public safety with an innovative ecosystem of life-saving tools. ABOUT THIS ROLE:

We are seeking an Autonomy Engineer to join our team and advance the navigation capabilities of our UAVs and public safety products. You will design, implement, and optimize real-time planning & perception algorithms that enable safe navigation across a wide range of scenarios. In this role, you will work across local trajectory generation, obstacle detection and avoidance, global route planning, and behavior planning. You will collaborate closely with state estimation & control, embedded software, live ops, and hardware teams to bring next-generation navigation solutions into production UAV platforms.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Research and design algorithms enabling safe UAV navigation.

 - Implement and optimize navigation algorithms on embedded processors for computational efficiency and system resiliency.

 - Work with sensor data to detect and avoid obstacles and environmental hazards.

 - Validate navigation performance through simulation, log replay, hardware-in-the-loop, and real-world flight testing in a variety of conditions.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration.

 - Contribute to technical strategy, define best practices, and help guide the evolution of the navigation and sensing stack as systems scale and mature.


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - PhD in Computer Science or a related field, or a Master's degree with a minimum of 2+ years of industry experience.

 - Strong programming skills in C++ and Python, with experience developing software for real physical systems.

 - Experience developing planning or perception systems for unmanned systems.


PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Familiarity with motion/path planning techniques such as RRT*, A*, or MPC-based trajectory optimization

 - Experience applying radar, computer vision, or deep learning for perception on resource-constrained edge hardware

 - Background in UAV reliability, redundancy, and autonomous flight considerations

 - Experience with ROS2, PX4, MAVSDK, and/or similar robotics middleware

 - Familiarity with safety-critical or certifiable software practices a plus

We are seeking an RF Test Engineer to design and develop test systems for validating the performance, reliability, and safety of our drones and public safety products. In this role, you will be responsible for creating automated and manual test setups, troubleshooting hardware issues, and ensuring our products meet rigorous quality standards. You will collaborate closely with hardware, firmware, and software teams to develop test procedures and improve product designs based on test results.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design and develop test hardware setups, fixtures, and automated testing systems for drone RF subsystems

 - Define and execute test plans to validate RF subsystems and how they interact with the rest of the drone .

 - Develop and maintain test scripts and software for automated testing.

 - Troubleshoot and diagnose hardware issues, providing recommendations for design improvements.

 - Conduct environmental and stress testing to ensure product durability in real-world conditions.

 - Work with engineering teams to refine designs based on test results and failure analysis.

 - Maintain detailed documentation of test procedures, results, and issue resolutions.

 - Ensure compliance with industry standards and safety regulations.
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, or a related field or relevant experience

 - 3+ years of experience in RF testing, preferably in UAVs, robotics, or consumer electronics.

 - Proficiency with RF test equipment such as VNAs, Spectrum Analyzers, Wideband Radio Communication testers, and signal analyzers.

 - Experience with automated test development using Python, LabVIEW, or similar tools.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills for electrical and RF systems.
   
   


PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Experience with LTE, GNSS and WiFi

 - Knowledge of regulatory testing and compliance for RF subsystems.

 - Familiarity with antenna design.

 - Experience with PCB debugging and hardware validation methodologies.
